description: An animal reproductive structure in which an embryo may develop is 'powea', usually enclosed by a membrane or shell and supplied with nourishment. The category includes unfertilised and soft shelled eggs alongside a bird's familiar hard shelled egg; viability is another fact. 'thinoe' (seed) belongs to plants, while 'thowia' (born) marks arrival.
articulatory_notes: A brief unvoiced lip closure releases into rounded 'o'. The lips stay rounded for voiced 'w' and stressed front 'e', then open into a separate final 'a' across the hiatus.
usage_notes: A developing egg can take optional 'lipha'; one counted as food or detached material can take 'themo'. A bare count leaves viability open.

semantic_domains.nature: an animal reproductive structure in which an embryo may develop
semantic_domains.physical: a protected structure that may contain nourishment and a developing embryo
